other_observers: I don't know the name of the person who found it, but I just heard from Brandon Percival that he was able to see the bird too.; Unknow, but reported to have been found yesterday, 03/04/2007
description: There were two large swans seen at Lake Minnequa in Pueblo. The one that is being documented is an adult in fresh alternate plumage. The fact that it was at times feeding with an adult Mute Swan was most interesting.  I concentrated on the feet, which were huge. I have a photo in case one is wanted. The body was white and large, the neck when not feeding was straight and white, and the head washed in orangy buff with a longish all black bill. The proximal end of the bill ended at a point by the all black eye. Head on the bill formed a "V" at its base. Looking at the profile of the bill, it was easy to see a rather straight edge, following it from the eye to the mandible. The profile of the top surface of the maxilla is straight-edged, not concave. The legs and feet were black, and as mentioned the size of the feet were enormous.Behaviors: Spent a lot of time feeding, tipped up with rear end sticking straight up and with the front half of the bird submerged. It pulled up large strands of some unknown aquatic vegetation. Although it mostly stayed away from the Mute Swan, there was a 5-minute period when the fed side by side.Call: none heardPlumage: alternate
